meeting: Headliner Breakout: EIP-8141 Frame Transaction - March 5, 2026

eoa_support_proposal.highlight: Derek proposes adding EOA support to 8141 via default account behavior
Default code applies only during frame tx execution; not persistent
Removes friction of 7702 account implementation selection and deployment tx
Strong support for EOA default account; improves adoption story significantly

technical_design.highlight: Default account could be precompile or EVM code; open question
VERIFY frames can target precompiles directly for native performance
Verify frames isolated; can be elided with validity proof for signature aggregation

mempool_and_standards.highlight: Mempool will enforce standard frame structures; similar to Bitcoin standardness
Derek: Standard frame structure needed for public mempool validation
7562-style mempool rules expected; allows-listed deployment proxies only
Frangio: Mempool rules need clarity in EIP; learned from 7702 experience

relationship_to_7702.highlight: 8141 doesn't obsolete 7702; both needed for smart account migration path
Felix: 7702 long-term strategy includes permanent delegation mechanism for PQ transition
Protocol removes signatures entirely; all validation becomes user-defined EVM computation

adoption_and_ux.highlight: Hardware wallet support critical; frame tx provides escape from 7702 implementation whitelisting
Daniel raises concerns: infinite flexibility creates security/UX friction like 4337/7702
Felix: Frame tx removes signatures from protocol; enables future-proof cryptographic flexibility

action_items.action: Finalize default EOA account format (precompile vs EVM code)
Write technical comparison: Frame tx vs Tempo tx addressing concerns
Review 8141 and voice support/concerns on ACD Execution call next Thursday
action_items.owner: All community members and client teams

decisions.decision: Strong consensus to add EOA support to 8141 via default account mechanism

targets.target: Next Thursday ACD Execution - headliner decision for Hegotá fork
